notes.anchor: a second consulship and a triumph
notes.note: Cicero recalls how Pompey ("our friend") had once been willing to procure Caesar a second consulship and a triumph for his Gallic conquests; the very honours now made impossible by the rupture between them, leaving Cicero loath to make Caesar an enemy.
The sum is given in the Latin as a sestertium figure (HS), the standard Roman unit of account; here a debt or transfer between the households being settled through Terentia.

A renewed Parthian invasion of Syria (a live threat since Crassus's defeat at Carrhae in 53 BC) might force Caesar and Pompey to compose their quarrel against the common foreign enemy; Cicero clutches at this faint hope of averting civil war.
